goal: Move the serving advisory and context-injection implementations behind the intelligence_serving namespace while preserving top-level import paths through compatibility wrappers.
constraints: Preserve existing import paths for callers and tests
Keep runtime and MCP serving at top level in this tranche to avoid a larger dependency rewrite
behavioral_changes: The real advisory and context-injection implementations now live under chip_labs.intelligence_serving.
The old top-level chip_advisor and chip_context_injector modules now act as compatibility wrappers.
The serving namespace API now imports advisory and context-injection behavior from the moved implementations directly.
verification_targets: PYTHONPATH=src python -c "from chip_labs.chip_advisor import AdvisoryRequest, advise_pre_action; from chip_labs.chip_context_injector import inject_context_for_task; from chip_labs.intelligence_serving.chip_advisor import advise_pre_action as moved_advise_pre_action; from chip_labs.intelligence_serving.chip_context_injector import inject_context_for_task as moved_inject_context_for_task; from chip_labs.intelligence_serving import AdvisoryRequest as api_AdvisoryRequest; print('serving-advisory-move-imports-ok')"
follow_up: The next serving move should likely target chip_runtime.py or chip_mcp_server.py, but only after reviewing their remaining cycles against the moved serving modules.
Keep compatibility wrappers until the serving surface is fully settled behind the namespace.
